meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Scale Your AI App: Analytics Setup Guide for Students
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your app fails. Learn how to implement advanced analytics to scale your student projects using Antigravity and Claude Code. Start now.
content.answer_block: Scaling fails when you fly blind. By integrating event-based telemetry, you transform raw traffic into actionable insights. Use Antigravity for automated instrumentation and Claude Code to refactor your codebase for observability, ensuring every user interaction is tracked, analyzed, and optimized for high-performance product growth.
content.intro:
You’ve built the MVP, but the growth has flatlined. It’s the classic student developer trap: you have code that runs, but you have no idea why users drop off after the first click. Without data, you are essentially guessing at your product's future, and in the world of AI, guessing is a one-way ticket to project abandonment.

But the bridge between a 'cool school project' and a 'scalable SaaS' isn't just better code—it's better visibility. By instrumenting your application early with a robust analytics stack, you stop debugging in the dark and start engineering for impact. Let’s turn your project into a data-driven engine.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one metric that defines value for your users (e.g., successful API generations).
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project structure and suggest a schema for tracking 'successful_generation' and 'user_churn' events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify key user interaction points in main.py and suggest event names'

steps.title: Automated Instrumentation
steps.description: Inject analytics middleware into your API routes to capture requests without manual boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable middleware wrapper that logs all request/response latency and tokens used.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply telemetry middleware to all FastAPI endpoints in /routes'

steps.title: Implementing Contextual Metadata
steps.description: Attach user IDs and prompt versions to every analytics event to enable cohort analysis later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my logging function to automatically append session_id and model_version to every log entry.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'update log_event function to include user metadata in global context'

steps.title: Error and Latency Tracking
steps.description: Capture system failures to identify bottlenecks before they impact your entire user base.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom error handler that tracks exception types and frequency to an external analytics sink.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'wrap database calls in error-tracking blocks that report to telemetry'

steps.title: Dashboard Visualization Setup
steps.description: Connect your event stream to a visualization layer to turn raw logs into clear growth trends.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL query or configuration file to visualize my telemetry data in a dashboard format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate dashboard schema for tracking daily active users and token consumption'

pro_tips: Always sample your event data at 10% for high-volume logs to save on cloud storage costs.
Never log raw user prompts containing PII; always hash your input strings before storage.
Correlate latency spikes with specific LLM model versions to identify which updates degrade performance.
Set up automated alerts for when your 'successful_generation' rate drops below your historical average.
Use 'event-id' headers in your frontend to trace client-side actions back to server-side logs.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 48 hours collecting data, then use Claude Code to perform a 'gap analysis' on your user drop-off points to iterate on your UI.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum. Your code deserves an audience, and your audience deserves a flawless experience. Implement these metrics today and watch your project transform from a simple script into a professional-grade application. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
